perturbation.original_text: In connection with the Prospective Purchase, the Company will provide GSEnergy with certain proprietary and commercially sensitive information and develop further such information with GSEnergy (all of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as "Information") which it wishes GSEnergy to maintain as confidential.
perturbation.changed_text: In connection with the Prospective Purchase, the New England Power Company will provide GSEnergy with certain proprietary and commercially sensitive information and develop further such information with GSEnergy (all of the foregoing hereinafter referred to as "Data") which it wishes GSEnergy to maintain as confidential.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Information" to "Data" in the context of what the New England Power Company will provide creates uncertainty. The contract later defines "Information", creating a contradiction of whether the initial provision of proprietary material is governed by the definition of "Information" or something different referred to as "Data."
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Information" shall mean and includes all information in whatever form, provided for the purpose of GSEnergy's review and analysis, but is not information which: (a) is in the public domain at the time of disclosure to GSEnergy; or (b) enters the public domain after disclosure to GSEnergy, except where such entry is the result of a breach by GSEnergy of this Agreement; or (c) was already in the possession of GSEnergy prior to disclosure to GSEnergy and was not then subject to an obligation of confidence; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to GSEnergy by a third person.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to proprietary information as "Information", which is consistent with the contradicted text's definition of "Information." The changed text, however, renames this proprietary information as "Data." Since the contract defines "Information" but does not define "Data", there's a contradiction regarding whether the proprietary material is governed by the definition of "Information" or something else. This creates a strong inconsistency because the obligations relating to "Information", as defined, may not apply to the proprietary material now referred to as "Data", leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: GSEnergy agrees that it will safeguard such Information maintaining it confidential and not using it except for the purposes of such review and analysis.
perturbation.changed_text: GSEnergy agrees that it will protect such material keeping it private and utilizing it exclusively for assessment.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "safeguard such Information maintaining it confidential and not using it except for the purposes of such review and analysis" with "protect such material keeping it private and utilizing it exclusively for assessment" introduces less formal terminology. "Information", "confidential", "review and analysis" are changed to "material", "private", and "assessment", respectively. This weakens the legal enforceability of the confidentiality agreement, as the scope of the confidential information and permitted uses are now less precisely defined and the original text strictly defines "Information", creating a contradiction about the definition of the exchanged information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Information" shall mean and includes all information in whatever form, provided for the purpose of GSEnergy's review and analysis, but is not information which: (a) is in the public domain at the time of disclosure to GSEnergy; or (b) enters the public domain after disclosure to GSEnergy, except where such entry is the result of a breach by GSEnergy of this Agreement; or (c) was already in the possession of GSEnergy prior to disclosure to GSEnergy and was not then subject to an obligation of confidence; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to GSEnergy by a third person.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses precise legal terminology such as 'safeguard such Information maintaining it confidential and not using it except for the purposes of such review and analysis.' The changed text replaces this with more informal language, like 'protect such material keeping it private and utilizing it exclusively for assessment.' The contradicted text provides a specific definition of 'Information.' By using the term 'material' instead of 'Information,' the changed text contradicts the defined scope of 'Information' in the contradicted text. The changed language weakens the enforceability of the confidentiality agreement, creating a direct conflict with the section defining 'Information' and its intended protection.

perturbation.original_text: GSEnergy agrees to maintain all Information in secrecy and confidence for a period of seven (7) years from the date of disclosure thereof, using the same degree of care as is used to protect its own Information.
perturbation.changed_text: GSEnergy agrees to hold all data discreet for a period of seven (7) years from the date it was shown to them, using the same level of attention as it uses for its own sensitive data.
perturbation.explanation: The original section commits GSEnergy to maintain "Information" in "secrecy and confidence". The modified section weakens this by replacing "Information" with "data" and "secrecy and confidence" with "hold all data discreet". 'Data' is less legally precise and doesn't explicitly include commercially sensitive information as suggested in the beginning of the contract creating an contradiction. The text defines 'Information' and not 'data', adding another layer of complexity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Information" shall mean and includes all information in whatever form, provided for the purpose of GSEnergy's review and analysis, but is not information which: (a) is in the public domain at the time of disclosure to GSEnergy; or (b) enters the public domain after disclosure to GSEnergy, except where such entry is the result of a breach by GSEnergy of this Agreement; or (c) was already in the possession of GSEnergy prior to disclosure to GSEnergy and was not then subject to an obligation of confidence; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to GSEnergy by a third person.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires GSEnergy to maintain 'Information' in 'secrecy and confidence'. The modified text uses the term 'data' instead of 'Information' and changes 'secrecy and confidence' to 'hold all data discreet'. The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Information' broadly. By replacing 'Information' with 'data' in the modified text, the obligation becomes less clear and might not include all the items initially covered by the definition of 'Information,' creating a contradiction, because 'data' is not defined. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' which occurs when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: If GSEnergy desires to use individuals not employed by GSEnergy, GSEnergy shall not disclose Information to these individuals without obtaining prior written approval from the Company and having appropriate non-disclosure agreements signed in form and substance satisfactory to the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: If GSEnergy wishes to involve consultants not on its payroll, GSEnergy should not reveal materials to them without getting permission beforehand from the Affiliate and making sure they sign confidentiality agreements that the Affiliate likes.
perturbation.explanation: The original section refers to "individuals not employed by GSEnergy" and disclosing "Information" to them, conditioned upon "prior written approval from the Company". This is replaced with "consultants not on its payroll", "materials", and "Affiliate"'s permission. The switch from 'Company' to 'Affiliate' adds ambiguity, as the definition of Affiliate is not stated to be the same as New England Power Company. Also switching, 'Information' to 'materials' creates uncertainty with the initial definition of "Information" provided.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Information" shall mean and includes all information in whatever form, provided for the purpose of GSEnergy's review and analysis, but is not information which: (a) is in the public domain at the time of disclosure to GSEnergy; or (b) enters the public domain after disclosure to GSEnergy, except where such entry is the result of a breach by GSEnergy of this Agreement; or (c) was already in the possession of GSEnergy prior to disclosure to GSEnergy and was not then subject to an obligation of confidence; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to GSEnergy by a third person.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Company' to 'Affiliate' and 'Information' to 'materials' creates a clear contradiction. The contradicted text defines "Information", while the changed text uses 'materials'. Since 'materials' is not defined, it is unclear whether it is included in the definition of 'Information', thus creating contradiction and uncertainty. The shift from requiring prior written approval from the 'Company' to the 'Affiliate' also creates ambiguity, as the definition of Affiliate is not stated to be the same as New England Power Company, leading to an in-text contradiction, as per the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: It will not constitute a violation of this Agreement for GSEnergy to disclose Information as required by a governmental body or a court of competent jurisdiction or as otherwise required by law, provided that the Company has been given notice of such requirement and been afforded a reasonable opportunity to contest it, if applicable.
perturbation.changed_text: It will not be a breach of this contract if GSEnergy has to give out protected items because the government or a court tells them to, or if the law says they have to. But, the Affiliate needs to know about it beforehand and get a chance to fight it if they can.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "violation of this Agreement" to "breach of this contract", "Information" to "protected items", "governmental body or a court of competent jurisdiction" to "the government or a court", and "Company" to "Affiliate" creates inconsistencies. Most importantly the the replacement of "Information" with "protected items", creates a contradiction because the text defines 'Information', but not 'protected items'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term "Information" shall mean and includes all information in whatever form, provided for the purpose of GSEnergy's review and analysis, but is not information which: (a) is in the public domain at the time of disclosure to GSEnergy; or (b) enters the public domain after disclosure to GSEnergy, except where such entry is the result of a breach by GSEnergy of this Agreement; or (c) was already in the possession of GSEnergy prior to disclosure to GSEnergy and was not then subject to an obligation of confidence; or (d) is rightfully disclosed to GSEnergy by a third person.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Information" to "protected items" creates a contradiction because the contract defines 'Information', but not 'protected items'. The contradicted text defines "Information", which is now rendered inconsistent with the changed text which uses "protected items". This constitutes a strong contradiction because the contract now uses a term that lacks definition, creating ambiguity and potentially conflicting obligations.
